title: Network Forensics
html:
Network Forensics

The discipline of analyzing captured network traffic to reconstruct behavior — what was visited, what was downloaded, what was anomalous. In the vault this is a single ten-screenshot study burst from June 9-11, 2025, all from the same OSU CS malware analysis lab week. Sits inside Yahir's broader spring–summer 2025 security cluster alongside mobile malware, surveillance state, and reverse engineering.

The June 2025 lab burst

Date Capture Focus
2025-06-09 2025 06 09 screenshot 2025 06 09 at 12 54 20 pm 69ac5054 initial Wireshark setup
2025-06-09 2025 06 09 screenshot 2025 06 09 at 1 09 50 pm fa1a25f4 filter syntax
2025-06-09 2025 06 09 screenshot 2025 06 09 at 1 09 58 pm 90b6652f follow-tcp-stream
2025-06-09 2025 06 09 screenshot 2025 06 09 at 1 13 19 pm d91bef48 HTTP traffic reconstruction
2025-06-10 2025 06 10 screenshot 2025 06 10 at 12 15 12 am ba57b115 late-night continuation
2025-06-10 2025 06 10 screenshot 2025 06 10 at 1 05 42 am f1abc915 VM lab capture (canonical)
2025-06-10 2025 06 10 screenshot 2025 06 10 at 1 54 26 am 79c874c6 1:54am — pulling an all-nighter
2025-06-11 2025 06 11 screenshot 2025 06 11 at 7 16 12 pm 4fc32534 next-evening write-up
2025-06-11 2025 06 11 screenshot 2025 06 11 at 7 34 06 pm 4f657cac analysis pass
2025-06-11 2025 06 11 screenshot 2025 06 11 at 7 45 30 pm f43b20a6 final report

Why it matters

This is one of the densest single-week study clusters in the corpus — 10 timestamps in 60 hours, all on one technical practice. Compare to the 2025 06 01 DVWA lab the week prior — back-to-back security course modules. Network-forensics + DVWA + mobile malware together form Yahir's summer 2025 security capstone era at OSU, the academic exit ramp toward Austin.
